questions.question: What is a force?
questions.questionThai: แรงคืออะไร?
questions.explanation: A force is a push or a pull that can change how an object moves.

questions.question: What is an example of a push force?
questions.questionThai: ตัวอย่างของแรงผลักคืออะไร?
questions.explanation: Kicking a ball is a push force because you move the ball away from you.

questions.question: What is gravity?
questions.questionThai: แรงโน้มถ่วงคืออะไร?
options: A force that pushes things up
A force that pulls things toward Earth
questions.explanation: Gravity is a force that pulls all objects toward the center of Earth.

questions.question: What is friction?
questions.questionThai: แรงเสียดทานคืออะไร?
options: A force that speeds things up
A force that slows down objects when surfaces rub together
questions.explanation: Friction is a force that opposes motion when two surfaces rub against each other.

questions.question: Which surface creates MORE friction?
questions.questionThai: พื้นผิวใดสร้างแรงเสียดทานมากกว่า?
questions.explanation: Rough surfaces like carpet create more friction than smooth surfaces like ice or glass.

questions.question: What makes a ball stop rolling?
questions.questionThai: อะไรทำให้ลูกบอลหยุดกลิ้ง?
options: Friction between the ball and ground
questions.explanation: Friction between the ball and the ground gradually slows the ball until it stops.

questions.question: What is motion?
questions.questionThai: การเคลื่อนที่คืออะไร?
options: The act of moving or changing position
questions.explanation: Motion is when something moves or changes its position.

questions.question: Which is an example of a pull force?
questions.questionThai: ข้อใดเป็นตัวอย่างของแรงดึง?
questions.explanation: Opening a drawer is a pull force because you move it toward you.

questions.question: What are balanced forces?
questions.questionThai: แรงสมดุลคืออะไร?
options: Forces that are equal and opposite, causing no change in motion
questions.explanation: Balanced forces are equal and opposite, so they cancel out and don't change an object's motion.

questions.question: What happens when forces are unbalanced?
questions.questionThai: เกิดอะไรขึ้นเมื่อแรงไม่สมดุล?
questions.explanation: Unbalanced forces cause objects to start moving, stop moving, speed up, slow down, or change direction.

questions.question: Why does a book stay still on a table?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมหนังสือถึงอยู่นิ่งบนโต๊ะ?
options: There are no forces on it
Gravity pulls down and the table pushes up with equal force
The book is too heavy to move
questions.explanation: The book stays still because gravity pulling down and the table pushing up are balanced forces.

questions.question: How does friction help you walk?
questions.questionThai: แรงเสียดทานช่วยคุณเดินได้อย่างไร?
options: It prevents your feet from slipping
questions.explanation: Friction between your shoes and the ground prevents your feet from slipping, allowing you to walk.

questions.question: In the reading passage, what made the go-kart slow down on gravel?
questions.questionThai: ในบทอ่าน อะไรทำให้โกคาร์ทช้าลงบนกรวด?
options: More friction from the rough surface
The kart ran out of energy
questions.explanation: The rough gravel surface created more friction than the smooth pavement, slowing the go-kart.

questions.question: Why did the go-kart speed up going downhill?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมโกคาร์ทถึงเร็วขึ้นเมื่อลงเนินเขา?
questions.explanation: Gravity pulled the go-kart down the hill, creating an unbalanced force that made it speed up.

questions.question: What is speed?
questions.questionThai: ความเร็วคืออะไร?
questions.explanation: Speed is how fast something is moving, usually measured in meters per second or kilometers per hour.

questions.question: How do brakes on a bicycle work?
questions.questionThai: เบรกจักรยานทำงานอย่างไร?
options: By creating friction that slows the wheels
questions.explanation: Brakes press pads against the wheels, creating friction that slows down and stops the bicycle.

questions.question: If a car is moving at constant speed in a straight line, what can you say about the forces on it?
questions.questionThai: ถ้ารถเคลื่อนที่ด้วยความเร็วคงที่ในเส้นตรง คุณพูดได้อะไรเกี่ยวกับแรงที่กระทำต่อมัน?
options: There are no forces on it
Only gravity is acting on it
questions.explanation: Constant speed in a straight line means balanced forces. The engine's push equals friction and air resistance.

questions.question: Why do we use oil in machines?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมเราใช้น้ำมันในเครื่องจักร?
options: To reduce friction between moving parts
questions.explanation: Oil is a lubricant that reduces friction between moving parts, preventing damage and making machines work better.

questions.question: What is the difference between mass and weight?
questions.questionThai: อะไรคือความแตกต่างระหว่างมวลและน้ำหนัก?
options: Mass is the amount of matter; weight is the force of gravity on that matter
questions.explanation: Mass is the amount of matter in an object (stays the same). Weight is how hard gravity pulls on that mass (changes with gravity).

questions.question: Why would a ball roll farther on a smooth floor than on carpet?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมลูกบอลถึงกลิ้งไปได้ไกลกว่าบนพื้นเรียบกว่าบนพรม?
options: The ball is heavier on smooth floors
Smooth floors have less friction, so less force opposes the ball's motion
questions.explanation: Smooth floors create less friction than carpet, so there is less force opposing the ball's motion, allowing it to roll farther.
